message.content:
Run CODEX (GPT-5.6) to gate the Q2/Q3 changes. Write /tmp/q2q3_gate.md then 'cd /tmp && timeout 200 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < /tmp/q2q3_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -20' (embed inline; medium; no sandbox flags).
Context: Q2 = PG_GENERATOR_MODEL code fallback standardized to '' at all sites (env sets it to glm-5.2 so runtime byte-identical); Q3 = lethal_retrieve rename per prior codex decision. Result: {"q2_sites_changed":4,"q3_renamed":false,"q3_skip_reason":"Task premise was incorrect: lethal_retrieve is not confined to the retired script. It is a live function defined at src/polaris_graph/wiki/mesh/retrieve/lethal.py:95 and imported by two ACTIVE test files (tests/integration/test_mesh_e2e.py:31 and tests/unit/test_mesh_lethal_retrieve.py:44) in addition to scripts/_retired_2026_06_14/pg_mesh_preflight.py:28. The name is also embedded in a persisted test filename (test_mesh_lethal_retrieve.py) and appears throughout .codex audit logs as a fixture path. This fails codex rename condition (b) (must NOT be referenced as an import / fixture path / persisted label elsewhere), so per the codex decision it is SKIPPED rather than renamed. Renaming would require touching two live test files and renaming a test file, well beyond the stated 'retired dir, low risk' scope.","collection_after":"16738 tests collected, 11 errors (== 16738/11, expected; the 11 are pre-existing env-dependent collection errors unchanged by these edits)"}. Verify: {"collection_ok":true,"oracle_matches":true,"oracle_sha":"9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98","characterization_ok":true,"no_stale_refs":true} (oracle byte-identical 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98).
Ask codex: "Is the PG_GENERATOR_MODEL '' standardization byte-safe (env-dominated), and is the lethal_retrieve rename reference-complete (or correctly skipped)? End with Q2Q3-SAFE or Q2Q3-REVISE."
Return verdict + points.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll gate the Q2/Q3 changes with CODEX. Let me first write the gate prompt file.

input.content:
You are gating two small code changes (Q2, Q3) in a Python codebase. Assess whether they are safe to land. Reason from the evidence provided; be adversarial about hidden risks. End your answer with exactly one token on its own line: Q2Q3-SAFE or Q2Q3-REVISE.

## Q2 — PG_GENERATOR_MODEL code fallback standardization

Change: the code-level fallback default for the `PG_GENERATOR_MODEL` config value was standardized to the empty string `''` at all 4 call sites where it was read.

- Result: `{"q2_sites_changed":4}`
- The deployed environment sets `PG_GENERATOR_MODEL=glm-5.2` via env, so the fallback `''` is never exercised at runtime — the effective value is byte-identical to before (env-dominated).
- The fallback only matters when the env var is unset (e.g. a fresh dev shell or a test without the env).

Question for Q2: Is standardizing the code fallback to `''` byte-safe given the env dominates at runtime? Consider: does changing an unset-env fallback from whatever-it-was to `''` risk a silent behavior change in any path that does NOT set the env (tests, CI, dev)? Is `''` a safe sentinel (empty string) vs. e.g. a previously-nonempty default that code downstream depended on?

## Q3 — lethal_retrieve rename (SKIPPED)

A prior codex decision authorized renaming `lethal_retrieve` ONLY if it was confined to a retired script and NOT referenced as an import / fixture path / persisted label elsewhere (rename condition b).

- Result: `{"q3_renamed":false, "q3_skip_reason":"lethal_retrieve is NOT confined to the retired script. It is a live function defined at src/polaris_graph/wiki/mesh/retrieve/lethal.py:95 and imported by two ACTIVE test files (tests/integration/test_mesh_e2e.py:31 and tests/unit/test_mesh_lethal_retrieve.py:44) in addition to scripts/_retired_2026_06_14/pg_mesh_preflight.py:28. The name is also embedded in a persisted test filename (test_mesh_lethal_retrieve.py) and appears throughout .codex audit logs as a fixture path. This fails rename condition (b), so per the codex decision it is SKIPPED rather than renamed."}`

Question for Q3: Given the evidence, is SKIPPING the rename the correct application of the prior codex decision (condition b: must NOT be referenced as import / fixture path / persisted label elsewhere)? Is the skip reference-complete (i.e., did they correctly identify that live references exist, making a rename out-of-scope)?

## Post-change verification (both Q2 and Q3)

- `{"collection_ok":true}` — pytest collection: 16738 tests collected, 11 errors (== 16738/11; the 11 are pre-existing env-dependent collection errors, unchanged by these edits).
- `{"oracle_matches":true, "oracle_sha":"9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98"}` — deterministic oracle output is byte-identical to the pre-change baseline.
- `{"characterization_ok":true}` — characterization tests pass.
- `{"no_stale_refs":true}` — no stale references left behind.

## Your task

Answer this precise question:

"Is the PG_GENERATOR_MODEL '' standardization byte-safe (env-dominated), and is the lethal_retrieve rename reference-complete (or correctly skipped)?"

Give a short reasoned verdict (a few bullet points), then end with exactly Q2Q3-SAFE or Q2Q3-REVISE on its own line.

content.text:
Interesting. `PG_GENERATOR_MODEL` doesn't appear anywhere in the current repo tree, and there's no diff/stash. The Q2/Q3 changes described in the task are not present in this working directory — this appears to be a gate on a report of changes made elsewhere (the box referenced in commits), not on the local tree. My job per the task is to run codex on the provided evidence and return the verdict + points, which I've done.

The codex concern is well-founded given the evidence as stated: the prompt itself characterized the prior fallback as "whatever-it-was" / "a previously-nonempty default," which does not establish that the unset-env path is byte-safe. The oracle SHA proves byte-identity only for the env-dominated (deployed) path, not for CI/dev/test paths where the env is unset. That is exactly the gap codex flagged.

I have the verdict and reasoning. Returning the structured result.

codex_points: Q3 (lethal_retrieve rename) was CORRECTLY SKIPPED. Codex confirms the skip is reference-complete: the live definition at src/polaris_graph/wiki/mesh/retrieve/lethal.py:95, two active test imports (tests/integration/test_mesh_e2e.py:31, tests/unit/test_mesh_lethal_retrieve.py:44), the persisted test filename, and .codex audit fixture paths all violate rename condition (b). A rename would be broader than the authorized 'retired dir, low risk' scope. No revision needed on Q3.

Q2 (PG_GENERATOR_MODEL '' standardization) is byte-safe ONLY in the deployed, env-dominated path where PG_GENERATOR_MODEL=glm-5.2. Codex declined to certify it byte-safe overall because the unset-env path (CI, tests, dev shells) is not proven safe.

The oracle SHA (9c0a3d...db98) establishes byte-identity for the env-dominated path only; it does NOT establish coverage of the unset-env fallback path. So 'oracle_matches:true' does not close the gap codex flagged.

Root gap: the prior code fallback value was described only as 'whatever-it-was' / 'previously-nonempty default'. Replacing an unknown prior nonempty default with '' could silently change model selection or trip downstream validation in any path that leaves the env unset. This is the crux of the REVISE.

Local verification note: PG_GENERATOR_MODEL does not appear anywhere in the current working tree (grep of *.py returns nothing, git diff/stash empty). The described Q2/Q3 edits are not present in /home/polaris/polaris_project locally -- this gate ran against the reported evidence, not a local diff.

Recommended remediation before re-gating Q2: identify the exact PRIOR fallback string at each of the 4 sites and confirm it was already '' (making the change a true no-op) OR add an unset-env test/oracle proving downstream behavior is unchanged when PG_GENERATOR_MODEL is absent. If the prior default was itself '', codex's concern dissolves and Q2 becomes SAFE.
